mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of rate-increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a structural deterioration in labor market tightness, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real savings and declines in housing starts indicate a structural shift in household demand away from interest-sensitive durables, depressing corporate earnings growth over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between savings and housing starts yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth consistently outpacing nominal GDP growth reflects supply-side efficiency gains that expand corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces negative forward returns during mid-cycle expansions, or the industrial production to GDP growth differential shows zero predictive power for monthly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods following CPI threshold breaches, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates a surge in labor force participation that dilutes wage growth, suppressing consumer demand and equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor market signal fails to predict negative quarterly returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy falling below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal savings rates provide direct funding for equity market participation and corporate capital expenditure, mechanically supporting positive forward stock market returns.
failure_condition: Real savings levels exhibit no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling estimation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle confirms demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, triggering rapid short-term equity corrections as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy remaining at or below 50%.
